alas: interjection. Used to express sorrow, regret, compassion, grief, resignation, or disappointment.

alms: noun. Something given to the poor as charity, such as money, clothing or food.

ana: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Andaqui.
anal: adjective. Of, related to, intended for or involving the anus.
ane: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Xârâcùù.
anlas: noun. Alternative spelling of anlace. 14th-15th centuries.
ansa: noun. The most protruding part of planetary rings as seen from a distance, perceived to project like handles on either side of the disc of
asea: adjective. on the ocean; on a boat on the ocean
ass: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ipulo.
elm: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eleme.
ens: noun. An entity or being; an existing thing, as opposed to a quality or attribute.
ess: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Central Siberian Yupik.
lane: noun. A road, street, or similar thoroughfare.
las: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gur Lama.
lase: verb. To use a laser beam on, as for cutting.
lases: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of lase
lass: noun. A girl; also, a young woman.

male: adjective. Belonging to the sex which typically produces sperm, or to the gender which is typically associated with it.
man: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mandingo.
mana: noun. Power, prestige; specifically, a form of supernatural energy in Polynesian religion that inheres in things or people.
manas: noun. The mind; that which distinguishes man from the animals.
mane: noun. Longer hair growth on back of neck of an animal, especially a horse or lion.

mel: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Central Melanau.
men: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mende (Sierra Leone).

mensal: adjective. Occurring once in a month; monthly.
mesa: noun. A flat area of land or plateau higher than other land, with one or more clifflike edges.

name: noun. Any nounal word or phrase which indicates a particular person, place, class, or thing.

salsa: noun. A spicy tomato sauce of Mexican origin, often including onions and hot peppers.
same: adjective. Not different or other; not another or others; not different as regards self; selfsame; identical.
sane: adjective. Being in a healthy condition; not deranged; thinking rationally.
sans: preposition. Without; lacking; especially with regard to something expected or with precedent.
sea: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Semai.
